California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 14, 1989

Bruce W. Robeck
Paralegal/Administrator
Political Reports Unit
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
770 L Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-360

Dear Mr. Robeck:

     This is to confirm the telephone advice given to you
concerning the Political Reform Act¹/ by this office on June 9,
1989 and June 12, 1989.

     Specifically, we advised the following:

     (1)  Contribution limitations for the fiscal year 1988-1989
(July 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989) apply only to contributions
received or contributions given from January 1, 1989 through
June 30, 1989.

     (2)  Fiscal year cumulations shown on campaign disclosure
statements for the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989
must include only those contributions which were received from
January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989.

     (3)  For purposes of the 1988-1989 fiscal year contribution
limitations, contributions to a candidate or officeholder which
were made by a recipient committee prior to January 1, 1989 are
not cumulated with contributions to the same person by the same
committee during the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989.
The answer is the same even if the candidate who received the

_______________________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-89-360
Page 2

funds has brought forward segregated 1988 funds which include
contributions from the same contributor.

(4) The committee receiving a contribution does not have to
determine if the contributor's sources of funds are in conformity
with the contribution limitations of Government Code Sections
85301 through 85305.

Our advice is based primarily upon Commission Regulation
18500 (copy enclosed) which essentially states that, for purposes
of the contribution limits set forth in the Act, the 1988-1989
fiscal year commences on January 1, 1989 and ends on June 30,
1989.

Should you have any additional questions, please contact me
at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Scott Hallabrin
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:SH:ld

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
SAN FRANCISCO

A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

FILE NUMBER

June 12, 1989

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Jeanne:

     This letter is to confirm in writing my understanding
of the telephone advice you provided to me on Friday, June 9,
1989, and Carla Wardlow provided to me on Friday, June 9, 1989,
and Scott Hallabrin provided to Judy Davis, Charles Bell and
myself on Monday, June 12, 1989. The client on whose behalf the
questions are asked is the California Medical Association PAC.

QUESTIONS

     1)   Are contribution limitations for the fiscal year 1988-
89 (July 1, 1988 through June 30, 1989) applied only to
contributions received or contributions given from January 1,
1989 through June 30, 1989?

     2)   For reporting purposes for the campaign disclosure
statements for the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989,
are the fiscal year cumulations to include only contributions
which were received from January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989?

     3)   Are contributions to a candidate or officeholder which
were made by a recipient committee prior to January 1, 1989
cumulated for purposes of fiscal year 1988-1989 contribution
limitations with any contributions to the same candidate or
officeholder by the same recipient committee during the period
January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989? Is the answer the same
even if the candidate who received the funds has brought forward
segregated 1988 funds which include contributions from the same
contributor?

     4)   Must the committee which receive a contribution
determine if the contributors' sources of funds are in conformity
to the contribution limitations of Government Code Sections
85301-85305?

SUMMARY OF ADVICE

1)     Contribution limitations for the fiscal year 1988-1989
(July 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989) apply only to contributions
received or contributions given from January 1, 1989 through June
30, 1989.

2)     For reporting purposes for the campaign disclosure
statements for the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989,
the fiscal year culmulations include only contributions which were
received from January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989.

3)     Contributions to a candidate or officeholder which were
made by a recipient committee prior to January 1, 1989 are not
cumulated for purposes of fiscal year 1988-1989 contribution
limitations with any contributions to the same candidate or
officeholder by the same recipient committee during the period
January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989.  The answer is the same
even if the candidate who received the funds has brought forward
segregated 1988 funds which include contributions from the same
contributor.

4)     The committee receiving a contribution does not have to
determine if the contributors' sources of funds are in conformity
to the contribution limitations of Government Code sections
85301-85305.

     If the above information conforms to your understanding
of the questions and the answers, please confirm.  We are
requesting to have an expedited response in order to properly
complete semi-annual disclosure requirements and to take such
actions as are required or permitted before the end of the fiscal
year.

     Thank you for your assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        BRUCE W. ROBECK
                                        Paralegal/Administrator
                                        Political Reports Unit
BWR:rh

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 19, 1989

Bruce W. Robeck
Paralegal/Administrator
Political Reports Unit
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-360

Dear Mr. Robeck:

We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on June 15, 1989. Your letter has
been assigned to Scott Hallabrin for response. If you have any
questions, you may contact him directly at (916) 322-5901.

If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

FILE NUMBER

June 12, 1989

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Jeanne:

This letter is to confirm in writing my understanding
of the telephone advice you provided to me on Friday, June 9, 1989,
and Carla Wardlow provided to me on Friday, June 9, 1989, and
Scott Hallabrin provided to Judy Davis, Charles Bell and myself on Monday, June 12, 1989. The client on whose behalf the questions are asked is the California Medical Association PAC.

QUESTIONS

1)    Are contribution limitations for the fiscal year 1988-89 (July 1, 1988 through June 30, 1989) applied only to contributions received or contributions given from January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989?

2)    For reporting purposes for the campaign disclosure statements for the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989, are the fiscal year cumulations to include only contributions which were received from January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989?

3)    Are contributions to a candidate or officeholder which were made by a recipient committee prior to January 1, 1989 cumulated for purposes of fiscal year 1988-1989 contribution limitations with any contributions to the same candidate or officeholder by the same recipient committee during the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989?  Is the answer the same even if the candidate who received the funds has brought forward segregated 1988 funds which include contributions from the same contributor?

4)    Must the committee which receive a contribution determine if the contributors' sources of funds are in conformity to the contribution limitations of Government Code Sections 85301-85305?

SUMMARY OF ADVICE

1)    Contribution limitations for the fiscal year 1988-1989
(July 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989) apply only to contributions
received or contributions given from January 1, 1989 through June
30, 1989.

2)    For reporting purposes for the campaign disclosure
statements for the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989,
the fiscal year cumulations include only contributions which were
received from January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989.

3)    Contributions to a candidate or officeholder which were
made by a recipient committee prior to January 1, 1989 are not
cumulated for purposes of fiscal year 1988-1989 contribution
limitations with any contributions to the same candidate or
officeholder by the same recipient committee during the period
January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989.  The answer is the same
even if the candidate who received the funds brought forward
segregated 1988 funds which include contributions from the same
contributor.

4)    The committee receiving a contribution does not have to
determine if the contributor's sources of funds are in conformity
to the contribution limitations of Government Code sections
85301-85305.

If the above information conforms to your understanding
of the questions and the answers, please confirm.  We are
requesting to have an expedited response in order to properly
complete semi-annual disclosure requirements and to take such
actions as are required or permitted before the end of the fiscal
year.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]
BRUCE W. ROBECK
Paralegal/Administrator
Political Reports Unit

QUESTION: 1) 
Are contribution limitations for the fiscal year 1988-
89 (July 1, 1988 through June 30, 1989) applied only to 
contributions received or contributions given from January 1, 
1989 through June 30, 1989? 
2) 
For reporting purposes for the campaign disclosure 
statements for the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989, 
are the fiscal year cumUlations to include only contributions 
which were received from January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989?

were made by a recipient committee prior to January 1, 1989 
cumulated for purposes of fiscal year 1988-1989 contribution 
limitations with any contributions to the same candidate or 
officeholder by the same recipient committee during the period 
January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989? 
Is the answer the same 
even if the candidate who received the funds has brought forward 
segregated 1988 funds which include contributions from the same 
contributor? 
4) 
Must the committee which receive a contribution 
determine if the contributors' sources of funds are in conformity 
to the contribution limitations of Government Code Sections 
85301-85305?

CONCLUSION: OF ADVICE 
1) 
Contribution limitations for the fiscal year 1988-1989 
(July 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989) apply only to contributions 
received or contributions given from January 1, 1989 through June 
30, 1989. 
2) 
For reporting purposes for the campaign disclosure 
statements for the period January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989, 
the fiscal year cumulations include only contributions which were 
received from January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989. 
3) 
Contributions to a candidate or officeholder which were 
made by a recipient committee prior to January 1, 1989 are not

The answer is the same 
even if the candidate who received the funds has brought forward

4) 
The committee receiving a contribution does not have to 
determine if the contributors' sources of funds are in conformity 
to the contribution limitations of Government Code sections 
85301-85305. 
If the above information conforms to your understanding 
of the questions and the answers, please confirm. 
We are 
requesting to have an expedited response in order to properly 
complete semi-annual disclosure requirements and to take such 
actions as are required or permitted before the end of the fiscal 
year.

Thank you for your assistance.
